channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: KOBAN SÜDVERS Adds CyberCube Analytics
item.description: “We are actively growing our cyber insurance portfolio and advancing our cyber strategy, creating strong alignment with CyberCube’s analytics capabilities,” comments Florian Traussnig, Managing Director for Risk and Insurance Technique, KOBAN SÜDVERS. “KOBAN SÜDVERS has demonstrated a clear commitment to advancing its cyber offering, and we are excited to support its ambitions with our analytics.” CyberCube Updates Account Manager for Global Cyber Expansion (Image source: KOBAN SÜDVERS homepage.) KOBAN SÜDVERS (Vienna) has become CyberCube ’s (San Francisco) first broker client in Austria.

item.title: Sapiens Names Kellen Petersen Americas Sales EVP
item.description: (Kellen Petersen, EVP, Sales, Americas, Sapiens. (Holon, Israel) has appointed Kellen Petersen EVP, Sales, Americas. Americas Sales Growth Petersen will lead sales across the Americas with responsibility for revenue growth and go-to-market execution.

item.title: Why Toyota RAV4s Are Suddenly the Most Coveted Used Cars in America
item.description: Most cars lose value the moment they leave a dealer’s lot. Used, late-model versions of the RAV4 hybrid often list for more than their original sticker prices, even with thousands of miles on their odometers. In some cases, they cost more than a new 2026 model fresh from the Toyota Motor Corp.

item.title: Hedge Funds Are Hiring Experts in Catastrophe Risk
item.description: Now, some of the world’s biggest hedge funds and banks want to incorporate the expertise of catastrophe-risk professionals across a broader chunk of the finance sector, as the skills required to model natural disasters become increasingly relevant for asset values in a hotter, more turbulent world. For hedge funds, the challenge is finding ILS experts who also have a nous for finance and investing. ILS—which stands for insurance-linked securities—is “a relatively new and emerging area among hedge funds,” he said.

item.title: USAA Not Done With Dividends: Florida Reforms Prompt $0.5B Payout
item.description: When USAA announced nearly $4 billion in policyholder dividends in December last year, the military insurer cited its financial discipline and the proactive steps of members to prevent losses among reasons for the payout. This week, USAA announced another forthcoming payout— a $500 million dividend for Florida auto policyholders—this time pointing to tort reform measures in the state as a driving force making the distribution possible. “Florida’s civil litigation and tort reforms have curbed legal system abuse, helping reduce the legal costs that were a significant driver of premium increases, enabling USAA to pass meaningful savings directly to members,” the statement said, calling out other states that are following suit—Georgia, Louisiana and New York.

item.title: Property rates more than adequate this year. But casualty faces wall of investor interest: Beaton, Ark
item.description: “So you can change a couple of things, in terms of the shape of your portfolio and what you are writing and what you aren’t writing, but things are sliding towards less profitable across the portfolio.” On what this might mean for investor interest in property catastrophe risks, in particular with reference to Ark’s own reinsurance sidecar structure, Beaton further stated that, “We still see it as more than adequate this year and we’d expect it to be adequate next year. This year we think profitability is still more than adequate, we think at those degrees of rate change the following year will be adequate.” Later in response to a question, Beaton went into more detail, “There’s a direction of travel, that’s the rate change and then there’s an underlying margin or profitability and we still see this year as above our hurdle returns in terms of profitability. While the hard market in property insurance and reinsurance has ended and the direction of travel for rates is generally down, Ian Beaton CEO of global specialty re/insurer Ark said he still feels there is rate adequacy this year and maybe into next.

item.title: Hannover Re US names Elena Strunk as Vice President, Medical Director
item.description: Elena Strunk as Vice President, Medical Director. Previously, she was Corporate Vice President, Medical Director at New York Life Insurance Company. Strunk will help advance Hannover Re’s underwriting and digital transformation initiatives, lead efforts to modernise medical assessment practices to reflect evolving medical knowledge, and strengthen the company’s medical oversight and governance capabilities.

item.title: Shrinking underwriting margins threaten US D&O liability space despite profitability: AM Best
item.description: “Despite generating solid direct underwriting results during the past few years, the competitive D&O marketplace is expected to become a little tighter in 2026, with underwriting margins likely to shrink,” said Christopher Graham, senior industry analyst, AM Best. While US Director & Officers’ (D&O) liability insurance remains profitable, premiums declined for a fourth straight year in 2025, reflecting heightened competition in the segment as warning signs loom over commercial line’s underwriting performance, according to a new AM Best report. “This might indicate an underlying deficiency that could lead to a downturn in D&O liability underwriting results over the near term,” said David Blades, associate director, AM Best.

item.title: People Moves: CorVel Names Scott as CEO and President
item.description: CorVel announced that Sarah Scott will be appointed CEO and President, effective July 1, 2026, succeeding Michael G. Scott has served CorVel for more than 26 years and currently is the Executive Vice President, Product and Corporate Services. Combs has served as President since 2017 and CEO since 2019, leading the company through significant growth and strategic development.

item.title: Markets/Coverages: Willis Expands Int’l Property Facility, With $60M Follow Capacity
item.description: Willis, a WTW business, announced the expansion of its international property facility, now able to offer up to US$60 million of follow capacity per placement. The automatic follow capacity, which is supported by Willis’ algorithmic digital platform Neuron, has also expanded with new markets. Our clients benefit from a streamlined placement approach, powered by our algorithmic digital trading platform Neuron, that’s particularly useful for creating competitive alternative options or completing placements and filling gaps in layered programs,” commented Edward Day, head of international property, Willis direct and facultative.

item.title: People Moves: Specialty MGA Taps Marsh’s Smithson to Head Global Financial Lines and Cyber Division; UIB Names Burns and Wilcox’s Webb as Divisional Director – Binders
item.description: Specialty MGA Taps Marsh’s Smithson to Head Global Financial Lines and Cyber Division Specialty MGA , a dedicated MGA platform that is part of MNK Group, has appointed Marcus Smithson as head of Financial Lines and Cyber, as it continues to scale its international specialty capabilities. Smithson, based out of the firm’s London office, will lead the existing underwriting team to accelerate the development of its global financial lines and cyber portfolio. “This appointment enhances our ability to develop a diversified, technically underwritten portfolio across D&O, professional indemnity, crime and cyber liability lines, and reflects our continued investment in specialist underwriting capability.” Specialty MGA focuses on lines of business, including but not limited to property, construction, energy, marine, construction, aviation, financial lines, accident & health and surety.

item.title: A Practical Launch Framework for Insurance Startup Programs
item.description: Insurance startups, MGAs, carriers, wholesalers, and brokers often struggle to launch new programs because the work is not organized clearly. Unclear roles and weak project discipline—not talent shortages—cause most insurance program launches to stall or fail. If we could restart many insurance program launches we have worked on or observed over the last decade, we would not begin by hiring more people or buying more technology.

item.title: Underwriting Needs Operating Models, Not Technology
item.description: Traditional insurers must rebuild their operating foundations before new technology can let them match the agility of MGAs and insurtechs. We talk a lot about growth, distribution, AI, data and digitization, but most of our operating models simply aren't built for the environment they're being asked to perform in. This was the backdrop to Send’s recent INFUSE webinar , where we explored what it really means to move from a traditional, linear underwriting model to something fit for 2026.

item.title: The Blind Spot in AI-Driven Loss Prevention
item.description: But every insurer building this infrastructure has a critical blind spot. Insurers deploy AI-driven tools to monitor and manage risks but lack systematic visibility into how well maintained the underlying assets are. The commercial insurance industry is moving decisively toward prevention.

item.title: Marsh partners with Kirontech on healthcare claims analysis
item.description: Marsh announced an exclusive UK collaboration with Cambridge-based health AI company Kirontech to help healthcare organizations analyze historic claims data, improve payment integrity, and identify opportunities to reduce waste and improve patient outcomes. Under the agreement, Marsh clients in corporate health trusts, private medical insurance claims environments, and NHS-commissioned care will gain access to Kirontech’s AI-driven analysis of medical treatment data and insurer-approved treatment plans. The offering combines Kirontech’s machine learning capabilities with Marsh’s healthcare consulting, governance, and risk expertise.

item.title: Walmart Canada launches free extended warranty program with Asurion
item.description: Walmart Canada has launched Walmart Extended Care, a new program powered by Asurion that provides customers purchasing eligible televisions, laptops, and desktop computers with an additional year of warranty coverage at no extra cost. The program includes two years of total defect coverage, with Walmart Extended Care extending protection beyond the manufacturer’s warranty period. The launch builds on Walmart Canada’s existing Walmart Protection Plan, also administered by Asurion, which offers paid extended coverage options for eligible electronics.

item.title: Nationwide names Andee Gravitt head of pet insurance sales
item.description: Nationwide has appointed Andee Gravitt as leader of pet insurance sales, effective June 8, reporting to Nationwide pet insurance president Joel Carnes. According to Carnes, Gravitt’s experience in sales leadership, client relationship management, and business development will support Nationwide’s growth ambitions in the pet insurance market. Gravitt most recently served as assistant vice president of Institutional Consultant Relations for Nationwide Retirement Solutions, where she led national consultant engagement strategy for the company’s employer-sponsored retirement plan business.

item.title: Suspects Indicted for Impersonating Shipping Carriers in $5M Multi-State Theft Ring
item.description: Eight members of a Northeast theft ring have been charged with fraud for impersonating legitimate shipping companies and stealing nearly $5 million in cheese, cigarettes, and copper from logistics sites in Pennsylvania, Virginia and New Jersey, according to a press release issued last week by the Manhattan District Attorney’s Office. “A theft ring this sophisticated, one that used hacked data to impersonate shipping companies and intercept cargo, doesn’t get dismantled without serious coordination across multiple levels of law enforcement. According to court documents and statements, the defendants operated the theft ring between October 2025 and April 2026.

item.title: Multi-Million Dollar Insurance Fraud Results in 4 Arrests
item.description: Four individuals were arrested following an investigation into an alleged automobile insurance fraud scheme in Iberia Parish, according to a recent announcement by the Louisiana State Police Insurance Fraud / Auto Theft Unit (IFAT). The investigation began after a criminal referral from Timber Creek Insurance Services and the Louisiana Department of Insurance Fraud Division regarding allegations of fraudulent insurance claims by the four suspects. Between April 30 and May 1, 2026, investigators obtained arrest warrants for all four suspects for charges related to automobile insurance fraud, a felony.

item.title: IGI secures Gift City license as part of global expansion
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA update on reforms to the UK Money Market Fund Regulation
item.description: On 15 May, the Government set out its expectation that it will lay legislation that will replace the UK Money Market Funds Regulation. We set out next steps on issuing new rules and guidance on Money Market Funds (MMFs), following Government plans to replace the current rules. Money Market Funds (MMFs) play an important role in the financial system.

item.title: FCA takes action against Neil Woodford and W4.0 for operating without authorisation
item.description: The FCA has started civil proceedings against Mr Neil Woodford and W4.0. The FCA alleges that Mr Woodford and W4.0 are providing regulated investment advice and making financial promotions through the subscription-based platform, www.w4pz.com , without authorisation. The FCA is seeking an injunction against Mr Woodford and W4.0 to stop them carrying on the potentially unlawful activities.

item.title: How Bigger ACVs Are Bringing Direct Sales Back To Vertical AI
item.description: As ACVs have grown in vertical AI, the go-to-market motion is changing too. Direct sales has historically only worked at true enterprise scale. With vertical AI ACVs frequently landing in the 6- or 7-figure range, founders now have room to invest meaningfully in winning each logo.

item.title: Price guidance lowered a second time for MPIUA’s $150m Mayflower Re 2026 cat bond
item.description: Price guidance has been lowered again for the latest catastrophe bond for the Massachusetts Property Insurance Underwriting Association (MPIUA), with the range of spreads on offer reduced a second time for both tranches of the $150 million Mayflower Re Ltd. Then, we reported last Friday that while the target size for this new Mayflower Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond remains unchanged at $150 million, the MPIUA was looking to capitalise on strong price execution in the cat bond market, with the price guidance lowered for both of the tranches of notes on offer. Now, we’ve learned that the price guidance for each tranche of notes has been lowered a second time, as the MPIUA aims to secure even more attractive price execution for its latest cat bond sponsorship.

item.title: Hamilton’s new sidecar a scalable capital solution, enhances casualty reinsurance support: CFO, Howie
item.description: Following the recent launch of Hamilton Insurance Group’s first casualty reinsurance sidecar, Craig Howie, Group Chief Financial Officer, explained that the sidecar enhances Hamilton’s ability to support casualty reinsurance underwriting through scalable and efficient capital solutions. “This sidecar enhances our ability to support casualty reinsurance underwriting through scalable and efficient capital solutions, and it also provides Hamilton with an additional source of fee income,” the CFO said. Chief Executive Officer of the firm, said that the company’s casualty reinsurance sidecar reflects a “proactive approach to capital and portfolio management.” “This structure allows Hamilton to support targeted casualty reinsurance growth while providing us with an additional source of fee income.

item.title: IGI to open a new branch in India’s GIFT City as it secures license
item.description: (IGI) has secured a license to operate in India’s Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City), the country’s first and only operational international financial services centre. By later this year, IGI plans to inaugurate its GIFT City branch, recruit a local India-based team, and commence operations. IGI’s India operation is a reinsurance branch of IGI’s UK subsidiary, International General Insurance Company (UK) Ltd.

item.title: Chalkias appointed as Everest’s Australia Country Head & CFO
item.description: Bermuda-based global reinsurance group Everest has appointed Peter Chalkias as Australia Country Head and Chief Financial Officer. Chalkias is one of Everest’s most recent appointments, following industry veteran Lisa Davis’ as Head of North America, Wholesale & Specialty in early May. He initially joined Everest Australia in 2013 as Chief Financial Officer.

item.title: Liberty Specialty Markets launches bespoke proposition for retail businesses
item.description: Liberty Specialty Markets (Liberty), part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, has announced the launch of a bespoke proposition for mid to upper-mid sized retail corporations. Stuart Sutherland, head of UK retail, Liberty Specialty Markets, said: “Liberty is focused on engaging with clients as a long-term risk partner. The proposition announced today brings that approach to mid and upper-mid retail businesses and is particularly timely as the mid-market drifts further toward commoditisation.

item.title: Miami Moves to Seize Part of Posh Island After Fuel Fight
item.description: Miami’s mayor is moving to forcibly acquire 10 acres of land on Fisher Island, one of the priciest ZIP codes in the US, from real estate developers who want to replace a maritime fuel facility with swanky new condominiums. The site is home to the Port of Miami’s fuel depot, a vital piece of infrastructure for the bustling cargo and cruise hub. The proposal was welcomed by island residents who see the facility as an eyesore and a potential threat to their health, but caused alarm in the cruise industry over the availability of fuel.

item.title: Thai Consumer Watchdog to File Civil Suit Against Volvo Cars’ Thai Unit, Official Says
item.description: Thailand’s consumer watchdog has approved the filing of a civil lawsuit against the local unit of Volvo Cars and its repair and maintenance unit, Scandinavian Auto Co. The Thai unit of Volvo Cars did not respond to a Reuters request for comment. The legal move comes after a meeting in Bangkok last month between Volvo Cars, the consumer protection board and customers failed to reach an agreement.

item.title: D&O Market Expected to Tighten Under Pressure, Says AM Best
item.description: Analysts at insurance rating firm AM Best said there are “warning signs” for the directors & offers liability market, leading to shrinking underwriting margins and a tighter environment for buyers in 2026. The IPO market rebounded in 2025 and has since leveled off somewhat due to economic uncertainty related to the Iran conflict, AM Best said. In a new report , AM Best said 2025 marked the fourth straight year of premium decline in the D&O liability line.

item.title: Aon appoints José María Navas as Chief Claims Officer for EMEA
item.description: Aon has appointed José María Navas as Chief Claims Officer for EMEA, as the broker continues to strengthen its regional claims capabilities amid growing complexity in the risk landscape. Tassarotti said: “José María’s appointment reflects our continued investment in delivering strong claims outcomes for clients across EMEA. José María’s appointment reflects the strength of our internal talent pipeline and our focus on differentiating Aon through leading claims capabilities across Risk Capital, from placement through to resolution.” Navas said: “I’m honoured to step into this role and to work alongside our teams to continue enhancing the claims experience for our clients.

item.title: INSTANDA launches AI-powered underwriting platform to streamline complex insurance workflows
item.description: The post INSTANDA launches AI-powered underwriting platform to streamline complex insurance workflows appeared first on Insurtech Insights . INSTANDA has launched INSTANDA Clear, an AI-supported underwriting and operations platform designed to help insurers manage complex, human-led workflows through a single governed environment. The launch marks a strategic expansion of INSTANDA’s no-code insurance technology offering, targeting operational bottlenecks in underwriting and claims processes where manual intervention, fragmented systems, and email-based collaboration continue to slow decision-making.

item.title: Convex launches tech-enabled MGA Kinetic Insurance Services for specialty risks
item.description: Convex Group has launched Kinetic Insurance Services, a technology-enabled managing general agent (MGA) focused on specialty and niche insurance risks, as the group looks to expand its delegated underwriting capabilities and create a new growth channel. Kinetic Insurance Services will operate as a wholly owned MGA targeting specialty business lines that complement, but remain distinct from, Convex’s core underwriting portfolio. Using our capability for writing delegated business we believe that this business will become a major part of Convex’s future growth.” Strategically, the launch underscores increasing interest among global carriers and reinsurers in MGA structures as a scalable route to accessing specialist risks, particularly as technology and delegated underwriting models reshape the specialty insurance market.

item.title: HUB creates AI sales innovation role
item.description: HUB International has appointed Faizal Mitha to the newly created role of Vice President of AI Sales Innovation, effective July 1, 2026, as the brokerage expands its use of artificial intelligence to support new business generation and organic growth. Most recently, he served as Chief Sales & Innovation Officer for HUB Canada Employee Benefits and Retirement, where he helped launch new services, including HUB Canada Persona Analysis, and led initiatives integrating insurtech capabilities into the business. Faizal has spent his career at the intersection of digital innovation and client service, and his new role builds on that experience.” – Marc Cohen, President and CEO of Hub.

item.title: Liberty rolls out regional retail insurance initiative across UK and Ireland
item.description: Liberty Specialty Markets has launched a new insurance proposition for mid-sized and upper-mid-sized retail businesses across the UK and Ireland following a pilot program. The offering is part of Liberty’s regional growth strategy and will be delivered through dedicated teams in Manchester, Bristol, and Dublin, supported by its London headquarters. We’re there for our clients’ worst day and, by engaging with them before a loss with our integrated underwriting, risk engineering and claims approach, we can deliver faster, better informed and more responsive support when it matters most.” – Stuart Sutherland, head of UK retail, Liberty Specialty Markets.

item.title: GoHealth files for Chapter 11
item.description: Medicare broker GoHealth announced that GoHealth and certain of its subsidiaries have voluntarily filed chapter 11 petitions with the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware. The Plan will transition ownership of the company to certain of GoHealth’s lenders, reinstate the preferred equity of GoHealth, Inc., provide for payment in full of trade payables and other ordinary course obligations, provide a cash payment to holders of GoHealth common equity, protect GoHealth’s critical relationships with customers and health insurance carriers, and position GoHealth for future success. GoHealth has already received votes in favor of the Plan from 100% of its lenders, over 60% of the holders of GoHealth, Inc.

item.title: Wamco to Pay $100 Million in SEC Settlement Over Leech Trades
item.description: reached a $100 million settlement with the Securities and Exchange Commission to resolve an investigation related to trading practices of its onetime star manager Ken Leech. The former co-chief investment officer at Wamco, Leech is charged with steering more than $600 million in gains to favored clients while forcing losses on others. Wamco said it agreed to the settlement without admitting any wrongdoing as a business decision that allows it “to put this matter behind it and focus fully on its clients.” “The DOJ has notified Western Asset that it is no longer a subject of its investigation and will take no further action,” the company said in a filing.

item.title: Aon Names Navas Chief Claims Officer for EMEA
item.description: Aon plc named José María Navas chief claims officer for Europe, the Middle East and Africa. Navas will lead the firm’s claims organization across EMEA, overseeing strategy, advocacy and claims preparation capabilities. Navas has nearly 25 years of experience with Aon, most recently leading claims in Spain.

item.title: The Benefits of an AI Medical Chronology
item.description: That’s where an AI medical chronology changes the game. A modern AI medical chronology uses the power of artificial intelligence (AI) to significantly speed up your claims workflow. With an AI medical chronology, thousands of pages of medical records can be automatically sorted, indexed, and stored in an easy to use timeline.

item.title: Lockton confirms Sandra Lee as Asia chief growth officer for risk solutions
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Markel promotes Jenny Choi to head of casualty for greater China
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aleksandra Kaczmarek returns to Coface as Asia Pacific head of sales
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Consumers warned about misleading car finance 'money tips' claims ads
item.description: Consumers are being warned to be wary of misleading car finance 'money tips' adverts issued by claims management companies (CMCs) and law firms on social media. If they choose to, it should be a genuine and well-informed choice, not one made because of a misleading advert.' Consumers do not need to use a CMC or a law firm to make a car finance claim – information on how to make a complaint for free is available on the FCA website . As part of the joint regulatory taskforce , the FCA has identified a growing number of adverts that appear to offer independent advice from an individual but are in fact paid promotions from CMCs and law firms encouraging people to sign up for motor finance claims.

item.title: AI in financial services: shaping our approach through industry engagement
item.description: We want industry to tell us: This helps us build an evidence-led view of AI in financial services. We recognise that AI can raise new and practical questions, and we want to help industry to tackle these. Given the pace of development, this good and poor practice needs to be grounded in the latest examples and answer the urgent questions industry has.

item.title: FCA secures confiscation order against Ponzi scheme fraudster
item.description: The FCA has secured a confiscation order of £452,286.80 against convicted fraudster Daniel Pugh. Only 19% of the funds collected from investors were traded and the scheme was, in effect, a Ponzi scheme, which was run with another individual. We will do everything in our power to deny them the profits from their crimes.' If Mr Pugh does not pay the confiscation order within 3 months, he faces a default prison sentence of up to 4 years and 9 months.

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending June 7th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending June 7th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending June 7th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Digital infrastructure expansion requires push in ILS and ART solutions: Aon
item.description: The rapid expansion of digital infrastructure creates exposures traditional insurance markets are struggling to absorb. This is particularly evident in sectors with concentrated values and long-dated demand, such as digital infrastructure. But alternative risk transfer (ART) solutions provide buyers with tools to reshape data center risk and create insurable structures where traditional capacity falls short, with insurance-linked securities emerging as a primary strategy, according to Aon.

item.title: Cat bond fund UCITS average 0.36% return in May. YTD hits 2.28%, 12-month 10.18%
item.description: UCITS catastrophe bond funds delivered an average return of 0.36% through the month of May, taking the year-to-date 2026 return through May 29th to 2.28%, while rolling twelve month performance declined slightly to 10.18%, according to the latest data from the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices . On the back of another positive month, the average year-to-date performance of the UCITS format catastrophe bond fund strategies rose from 1.91% at May 1st to now 2.18% as of May 29th 2026, the latest data available. The lower-risk cohort of UCITS cat bond funds delivered an average return of 0.24% for the period May 1st to May 29th inclusive, while the higher-risk cat bond fund strategies averaged a 0.42% return.

item.title: Willis expands international property facility with up to $60m follow capacity per placement
item.description: Willis, a WTW business, has announced the expansion of its international property facility, boosting its follow capacity to up to USD 60 million per placement. Edward Day, head of international property, Willis direct and facultative, said: “Since launching the facility in 2024, we’ve seen strong, positive engagement from the market. The automatic follow capacity, which is supported by Willis’ algorithmic digital platform Neuron, has also expanded with new markets.
